method_card.parent_question: How can chunked/source-span LLM methods preserve source grounding while still supporting useful abstraction?
intended_inputs: second operator-approved source excerpt selected from the local pharm box-trades transcript
metadata-safe source hash and source ref for the selected excerpt
chunked source segments with the pilot 003 source-span precision expectations
DeepSeek V4 Pro with thinking disabled
pre-recorded prompt and model configuration metadata
known_risks: This is a proposed live LLM pilot planning record, not a completed run.
The second source may expose repeatability limits that were hidden by the first source.
Source selection can bias the repeat if the operator-approved excerpt is unusually clean or unusually fragmented.
Tighter source-span hints may create false precision if broad support is not labeled honestly.
The narrowed contract may preserve grounding while retaining limited abstraction.
Prompt or trace handling may accidentally include raw source material if the privacy boundary is not followed.
